page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate that the ecs.g6.xlarge delivers modest processing capabilities. In stress-ng tests, both single-core and multi-core CPU performance fall into the bottom 25% tier. Similarly, memory bandwidth tests show weak performance for large block reads and writes, though the PassMark memory score achieves an average rating. Cryptographic and compression tasks, such as single-threaded gzip and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, also register in the lower performance brackets.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.g6.xlarge provides a dedicated resource allocation without the shared-core limitations of burstable instances, making it a predictable option for workloads that do not require high-performance compute. Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and baseline network performance, this instance is suitable for general-purpose applications, lightweight web serving, development environments, and basic database operations that do not demand high IOPS or extreme memory throughput.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g6.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor at 2.5 GHz, paired with 16.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under the KVM hypervisor, this instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark results place its CPU and memory read/write performance in the lower tiers, though its PassMark memory score remains average. The processor supports AVX-512 and AES instructions, which assist with basic cryptographic and vector operations. Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the ecs.g6.xlarge is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring dedicated resources without high compute demands. It is best suited for general-purpose tasks, lightweight web serving, and development environments.
